source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 适度的; 中等的 average in amount, intensity, quality, or degree: drink moderate amounts of coffee 38° yn 4 adj. (政治立场 ) 不极端的; 温和的 (of a person, party, or policy) not radical or excessively right- or left-wing: a moderate reform program 温和的改革计划 v.t. ite; 缓和; 使适中 make or become less extreme, intense, rigorous, or violent: moderate the increasing demands for more building space 缓和对更多建筑用地日益增长的需求 | moderate one's pace 减慢步速近义词 temper

headline.core_en: not extreme; to make something less extreme

familiar_bridge.summary: medium 是“中等”，moderate 多了一层“不走极端、控制在合适范围内”。

core.main: moderate 可以形容数量、强度或政治立场“适中”，作动词时表示把过强的东西压下来、缓和。

senses.label: 适度的；中等的
senses.text: 数量、强度、速度或程度不过高也不过低。

senses.label: 温和的；不极端的
senses.text: 人、政策或观点不激进。

senses.label: 缓和；节制
senses.text: 让要求、语气、速度或冲突变得不那么强烈。

usage_groups.group_title: 数量或强度保持在适中范围
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 不是 very high，也不是 very low，而是 controlled and reasonable。

examples.sentence: Doctors often recommend moderate exercise rather than complete rest.
examples.translation: 医生常建议适度运动，而不是完全休息。
examples.teaching_note: moderate exercise 强调强度适中。

usage_groups.group_title: 观点或政策不走极端
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 这里强调观点或政策不走极端。

examples.sentence: The proposal won support because it was moderate enough to satisfy both sides.
examples.translation: 这个提案获得支持，因为它足够温和，能让双方接受。
examples.teaching_note: 这里 moderate 不是“中等数量”，而是“不极端”。

usage_groups.group_title: 把过强的反应或要求缓和下来
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 作动词，表示 reduce the force or intensity。

examples.sentence: The mediator tried to moderate the increasingly hostile debate.
examples.translation: 调解人试图缓和越来越敌对的争论。
examples.teaching_note: moderate 作动词时有“降温”的感觉。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The room was medium in size.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: medium 只说大小或程度中等；moderate 还常带“适度、不过分”的判断。
reasonable 更日常，moderate 更常用于程度、政策或立场。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: His temperate response helped calm the meeting.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 他温和克制的回应帮助会议平静下来。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: temperate 更强调克制和不过激。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The article offered a restrained criticism of the policy.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 文章对政策作了克制的批评。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: restrained 常形容表达方式被有意控制。

antonyms.example_sentence: The group rejected extreme solutions.
antonyms.example_translation: 这个团体拒绝极端方案。
antonyms.difference_note: extreme 正好与 moderate 的“不极端”相对。

antonyms.example_sentence: The senator opposed the radical reform plan.
antonyms.example_translation: 这位参议员反对激进改革方案。
antonyms.difference_note: radical 常用于政治或制度改革语境。

confusable_words.example_sentence: A neutral official mediated the dispute.
confusable_words.example_translation: 一名中立官员调解了这场争端。
confusable_words.difference_note: mediate 是在双方之间调解；moderate 是使强度降低或不过激。

extension.root_affix: moderate 和 mode / measure 的感觉有关：都指某种限度或尺度。

word_family.example_sentence: The doctor advised moderation in diet and exercise.

word_family.note: 常用于讨论、论坛或辩论。
word_family.example_sentence: The moderator kept the debate focused.

related_words.example_sentence: Experience tempered his enthusiasm with caution.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj， 确定不移的; 强有力的 firm and unchangeable: a cast-iron conservative 死硬的保守派 | a cast-iron rule 硬性规定

headline.core_en: very firm, strict, or certain

familiar_bridge.summary: iron 是铁；cast-iron 像“铸铁一样硬”，所以用来形容规则、承诺或立场很难改变。

core.main: cast-iron 强调 firm and unchangeable：规定很硬、证据很强、立场很死。

senses.label: 坚定不变的；强硬的
senses.text: 形容 rule, commitment, belief, conservative 等非常坚决。

senses.label: 铁定的；可靠的
senses.text: 形容 alibi, guarantee, proof 等很难被推翻。

usage_groups.group_title: 规则、承诺或立场很硬

examples.sentence: The school imposed a cast-iron rule against cheating.
examples.translation: 学校制定了一条严厉而不可动摇的反作弊规定。
examples.teaching_note: cast-iron rule 强调规则很硬。

usage_groups.group_title: 证据或保证非常可靠
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 这里强调证据或保证非常可靠。

examples.sentence: The suspect had a cast-iron alibi for the night of the crime.
examples.translation: 嫌疑人在案发当晚有无可动摇的不在场证明。
examples.teaching_note: cast-iron alibi 是固定感很强的搭配。

basic_synonyms.difference_note: firm 是常见词；cast-iron 语气更硬。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The lab follows strict safety rules.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 实验室遵守严格的安全规则。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: strict 常形容规则要求；cast-iron 更强调不可改变。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The policy was criticized as inflexible.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这项政策被批评为不够灵活。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: inflexible 带负面，强调不能调整。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The evidence seemed unassailable.

antonyms.example_sentence: The schedule is flexible.
antonyms.difference_note: flexible 与 cast-iron 的“不可变通”相反。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The contract contained an ironclad confidentiality clause.
confusable_words.example_translation: 合同包含严密的保密条款。
confusable_words.difference_note: ironclad 与 cast-iron 很接近，在美式英语中更常见。

extension.root_affix: cast iron 是铸铁；形容词 cast-iron 借这个物理硬度表示“很硬、不可动摇”。

word_family.note: 同样借 iron 表示坚固。
word_family.example_sentence: The company wanted an ironclad agreement.

related_words.example_sentence: An inflexible rule can create unfair results.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. (JHE AMAA) 彬彬有礼的; 殷勤的 (of a man or his behavior) courteous and gallant, especially toward women: chivalrous behavior jk BL

headline.core_cn: 彬彬有礼的；骑士般殷勤的
headline.core_en: courteous, honorable, and gallant

familiar_bridge.summary: chivalry 原指骑士精神；chivalrous 就是举止有礼、保护弱者、带一点传统绅士感。

core.main: chivalrous 不只是 polite，它还带有 honor, courtesy, gallantry 的感觉，常形容行为而不是普通礼貌。

senses.label: 彬彬有礼的；殷勤的
senses.text: 对人，尤其传统语境中对女性，表现出礼貌和照顾。

senses.label: 有骑士精神的；高尚的
senses.text: 强调 honor 和保护弱者的道德姿态。

usage_groups.group_title: 行为礼貌而殷勤
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 比 polite 更带传统绅士感。

examples.sentence: His chivalrous gesture seemed old-fashioned but sincere.
examples.translation: 他那种彬彬有礼的举动显得有些传统，但很真诚。
examples.teaching_note: chivalrous gesture 常带“骑士式殷勤”的色彩。

usage_groups.group_title: 在冲突中表现得高尚体面
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 不趁人之危，带 honor 的道德感。

examples.sentence: The captain was chivalrous toward the defeated enemy.
examples.translation: 这位队长对战败的敌人表现得高尚而有风度。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: He was polite to everyone at the meeting.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 他在会议上对每个人都很礼貌。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: polite 更普通；chivalrous 更有传统骑士风度。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The staff were courteous to the visitors.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 工作人员对访客彬彬有礼。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: His gallant conduct impressed the court.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 他的勇敢而有风度的行为打动了法庭。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: gallant 与 chivalrous 共享骑士感。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: She made an honorable decision under pressure.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 她在压力下作出了体面的决定。

antonyms.example_sentence: His rude remark embarrassed the host.
antonyms.example_translation: 他的粗鲁评论让主人尴尬。

antonyms.example_sentence: The official resigned after a dishonorable act.
antonyms.example_translation: 这名官员因不光彩行为辞职。

confusable_words.meaning_cn: 沙文主义的；大男子主义的
confusable_words.example_sentence: The comment sounded chauvinistic rather than respectful.
confusable_words.example_translation: 这句话听起来像大男子主义，而不是尊重。
confusable_words.difference_note: chivalrous 是尊重与礼貌；chauvinistic 是优越感或偏见。

extension.root_affix: chivalrous 来自 chivalry，原本和骑士制度、骑士精神有关。

word_family.example_sentence: He chivalrously offered his seat to the elderly passenger.

related_words.example_sentence: The story praises the soldier's gallantry.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 徒劳的; 没有用的 (of a task or undertaking) ineffectual; useless: Remonstrating with him seems ever to have been a bootless task. 向他抗议根本没有任何用

headline.core_en: useless or without result

familiar_bridge.summary: bootless 可以直接和 useless / futile 连接：努力做了，但没有结果。

core.main: bootless 常形容 efforts, attempts, pleas 之类“花了力气却没有效果”的行动。

senses.label: 徒劳的；无益的
senses.text: 行动、请求或努力没有带来想要的结果。

usage_groups.group_title: 努力或请求没有结果
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 这里强调努力或请求没有结果。

examples.sentence: After hours of bootless searching, the hikers turned back.
examples.translation: 徒劳地搜寻了几个小时后，徒步者折返了。
examples.teaching_note: bootless searching 表示搜了但没结果。

examples.sentence: His appeal to the committee proved bootless.
examples.translation: 他向委员会申诉，结果徒劳无功。
examples.teaching_note: proved bootless 是很自然的书面搭配。

basic_synonyms.difference_note: useless 更普通，可用于物品；bootless 多用于努力或行动。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: It was futile to argue with the dictator.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 和独裁者争辩是徒劳的。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: futile 是常见 GRE 近义。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: All their efforts were in vain.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 他们所有努力都白费了。

antonyms.example_sentence: The new treatment was effective.
The meeting was productive.

confusable_words.difference_note: 拼写相近但无关；bootless 不是“没有靴子”。

extension.root_affix: bootless 中的 -less 表示“没有”；这里不是 boots，而是较古的 boot = benefit，所以 bootless 是“没有益处”。

headline.core_en: to take part in, attract attention, or become involved

familiar_bridge.summary: engage 和 involvement 有关：人被拉进一件事，注意力被抓住，或双方进入互动。

core.main: engage 是多义词，但底层都是“进入某种关系或活动”：参与、吸引、雇用、交战。

senses.label: 参与；从事
senses.text: engage in an activity。

senses.label: 吸引注意或兴趣

senses.label: 与……交战或互动

usage_groups.group_title: 参与某项活动或讨论
usage_groups.meaning_focus: engage in 表示 actively take part。

examples.sentence: Students were encouraged to engage in open debate.
examples.translation: 学生被鼓励参与开放辩论。
examples.teaching_note: engage in 强调主动参与。

usage_groups.group_title: 吸引注意力或兴趣
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 材料或活动让人愿意投入。

examples.sentence: The opening scene immediately engaged the audience.
examples.translation: 开场场景立刻吸引了观众。
examples.teaching_note: engage the audience 表示抓住观众注意力。

usage_groups.group_title: 与对方进入冲突或互动
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 军事上可表示交战，也可表示与人交流。

examples.sentence: The army refused to engage the enemy on unfamiliar terrain.
examples.translation: 军队拒绝在陌生地形上与敌军交战。
examples.teaching_note: engage the enemy 是军事用法。

basic_synonyms.example_translation: 这个项目涉及几个部门。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: involve 强调被牵涉；engage 更强调主动投入或吸引。

basic_synonyms.example_translation: 这个故事让孩子们感兴趣。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: interest 只说引起兴趣；engage 更有投入感。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Many residents participated in the survey.
The speaker captivated the audience.

antonyms.example_sentence: The group withdrew from the talks.
antonyms.difference_note: withdraw 与 engage in 的参与相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The soldiers disengaged from combat.

confusable_words.example_sentence: She is engaged in medical research.
confusable_words.difference_note: engaged 可由 engage 派生，但也有“订婚”义。

extension.root_affix: engage 的词族围绕 involvement / commitment。

word_family.example_sentence: Student engagement increased after the new course design.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 有吸引力的; 迷人的 charming and attractive: an engaging speaker 吸引人的演说家 | an engaging smile 迷人的微笑近义词 intriguing, entertaining

headline.core_en: pleasant and attractive in a way that holds attention

familiar_bridge.summary: engaging 来自 engage：能把人的注意力“拉进去”的东西，就是 engaging。

core.main: engaging 常形容 person, style, story, lecture，让人愿意继续看、听或交流。

senses.label: 迷人的；吸引人的
senses.text: 友好、有趣或有魅力，能抓住注意力。

usage_groups.group_title: 人、风格或内容让人愿意投入

examples.sentence: The professor made a difficult topic engaging for first-year students.
examples.translation: 这位教授把一个困难话题讲得让一年级学生也愿意听。
examples.teaching_note: engaging 强调能吸引人投入。

examples.sentence: Her engaging manner made even skeptical guests feel welcome.
examples.translation: 她亲切迷人的举止让连持怀疑态度的客人也感到受欢迎。
examples.teaching_note: engaging manner 常形容人的交流风格。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The book is interesting from the first page.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 这本书从第一页起就很有趣。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: interesting 更普通；engaging 更强调抓住注意力。
pleasant 不一定吸引人投入。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The documentary was captivating.
The child had a winsome smile.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这个孩子有讨人喜欢的笑容。

antonyms.example_sentence: The lecture was dull and hard to follow.
antonyms.difference_note: dull 与 engaging 直接相反。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The students were engaged in the task.
